channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment centrifuges. According to a new report published by SentinelOne, the previously undocumented cyber sabotage framework dates back to 2005, primarily targeting high-precision calculation software to tamper
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets May 2026 Federal Deadline
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added four vulnerabilities impacting SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server, and D-Link DIR-823X series routers to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is below -
CVE-2024-57726 (CVSS score: 9.9) - A missing authorization vulnerability in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: How Squid Survived Extinction Events
item.description: <p>Science <a href="https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/03/260331001100.htm">news</a>:</p> <blockquote><p>Scientists have finally cracked a long-standing mystery about squid and cuttlefish evolution by analyzing newly sequenced genomes alongside global datasets. The research reveals that these bizarre, intelligent creatures likely originated deep in the ocean over 100 million years ago, surviving mass extinction events by retreating into oxygen-rich deep-sea refuges. For millions of years, their evolution barely changed&#8212;until a dramatic post-extinction boom sparked rapid diversification as they moved into new shallow-water habitats. ...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: FIRESTARTER Backdoor Hit Federal Cisco Firepower Device, Survives Security Patches
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has revealed that an unnamed federal civilian agency's Cisco Firepower device running Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) software was compromised in September 2025 with a new malware called FIRESTARTER. FIRESTARTER, per CISA and the U.K.'s National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC), is assessed to be a backdoor designed for remote access
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: US Busts Myanmar Ring Targeting US Citizens in Financial Fraud
item.description: Some 29 people were charged, including a Cambodian senator, and authorities seized more than 500 Web domains tied to fake investment sites.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Glasswing Secured the Code. The Rest of Your Stack Is Still on You
item.description: Forgotten integrations, shadow IT, SaaS, and now shadow AI and agents are everywhere, and attackers don't need sophisticated AI models to take advantage.
item.dc:creator: Ron Peled

item.title: NASA Employees Duped in Chinese Phishing Scheme Targeting U.S. Defense Software
item.description: The Office of Inspector General (OIG) of the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has revealed how a Chinese national posed as a U.S. researcher as part of a spear-phishing campaign to obtain sensitive information from the space agency, as well as from government entities, universities, and private companies, in violation of export control laws. "For years, NASA employees
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Corporate Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from April 20–24.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-breaches-and-corporate-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Corporate Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
item.description: In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: UK Biobank Data Breach: Health Data of 500,000 Listed for Sale in China
item.description: UK government Minister confirms that breached health records of UK Biobank volunteers were up for sale on Chinese ecommerce platforms before being removed

item.title: North Korea's Lazarus Targets macOS Users via ClickFix
item.description: Lazarus continues leveraging ClickFix for initial access and data theft, in this case, against Mac-centric organizations and their high-value leaders.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: AI Rush is Reviving Old Cybersecurity Mistakes, Mandiant VP Warns
item.description: AI tools are not just creating new vulnerabilities, they are reviving old security failures, warned Jurgen Kutscher, VP of Mandiant Consulting

item.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
item.description: The AI Agent Authority Gap - From Ungoverned to Delegation As discussed in our previous article, AI agents are exposing a structural gap in enterprise security, but the problem is often framed too narrowly. The issue is not simply that agents are new actors. It is that agents are delegated actors. They do not emerge with independent authority. They are triggered, invoked, provisioned, or
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 26 FakeWallet Apps Found on Apple App Store Targeting Crypto Seed Phrases
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious apps on the Apple App Store that impersonate popular cryptocurrency wallets in an attempt to steal recovery phrases and private keys since at least fall 2025. "Once launched, these apps redirect users to browser pages designed to look similar to the App Store and distribute trojanized versions of legitimate wallets," Kaspersky
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What Is Cloud Security? A 2026 Guide
item.description: <p>Learn what cloud security is, why it matters in 2026, and the best practices for protecting data, identities, workloads, and cloud infrastructure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/what-is-cloud-security/">What Is Cloud Security? A 2026 Guide</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Sue Poremba

item.title: Hiding Bluetooth Trackers in Mail
item.description: <p>It was used to <a href="https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/cyber-security/bluetooth-tracker-hidden-in-a-postcard-and-mailed-to-a-warship-exposed-its-location-a-eur5-gadget-put-a-eur500-million-dutch-ship-at-risk-for-24-hours">track</a> a Dutch naval ship:</p> <blockquote><p>Dutch journalist Just Vervaart, working for regional media network Omroep Gelderland, followed the directions posted on the Dutch government website and mailed a postcard with a hidden tracker inside. Because of this, they were able to track the ship for about a day, watching it sail from Heraklion, Crete, before it turned towards Cyprus. While it only showed the location of that one vessel, knowing that it was part of a carrier strike group sailing in the Mediterranean could potentially put the entire fleet at risk...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Tropic Trooper Uses Trojanized SumatraPDF and GitHub to Deploy AdaptixC2
item.description: Chinese-speaking individuals are the target of a new campaign that uses a trojanized version of SumatraPDF reader to deploy the AdaptixC2 Beacon post-exploitation agent and ultimately facilitate the abuse of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) tunnels for remote access. Zscaler ThreatLabz, which discovered the campaign last month, has attributed it with high confidence to Tropic Trooper (aka
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The calm before the ransom: What you see is not all there is
item.description: A breach claims the systems as well as the confidence that was, in retrospect, a major vulnerability

item.title: Npm Supply Chain Malware Attack Targets Developers With Worm-Like Propagation
item.description: Malicious npm packages spread via worm-like propagation and steal developer credentials

item.title: LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 Flaw Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in LMDeploy, an open-source toolkit for compressing, deploying, and serving LLMs, has come under active exploitation in the wild less than 13 hours after its public disclosure. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-33626 (CVSS score: 7.5), relates to a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability that could be exploited to access sensitive data. "A server-side
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Tropic Trooper APT Takes Aim at Home Routers, Japanese Targets
item.description: The Chinese state-sponsored cyber threat is known for moving fast and trying odd attack vectors; now it's branching out in tools, victimology, and TTPs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Seals

item.title: Chinese APT Abuses Multiple Cloud Tools to Spy on Mongolia
item.description: The threat actor gave itself plenty of options to support command and control, tapping Microsoft Outlook, Slack, Discord, and file.io for online espionage.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: China-Backed Hackers Are Industrializing Botnets
item.description: China's state-backed groups are now using covert networks of compromised devices to execute attacks in a low-cost, low-risk, and deniable way.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
item.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised hosts. "As with many other intrusions in recent years, UNC6692 relied heavily on impersonating IT help desk employees, convincing their victim to accept a Microsoft Teams chat invitation from an account
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Favors General-Purpose Gemini Models Over Cybersecurity‑Specific AI
item.description: Google Cloud’s COO advocated for combining general-purpose frontier large language models with task-specific AI agents

item.title: Clear market trend for software providers to help with AI: Proofpoint CEO
Apple Fixes iPhone Bug After FBI Retrieved Signal Messages
item.description: <p>Apple patched an iPhone notification bug that let deleted messages linger in system storage, closing a privacy gap exposed by an FBI Signal case.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-fixes-iphone-notification-bug-fbi-signal-messages/">Apple Fixes iPhone Bug After FBI Retrieved Signal Messages</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: The Context Gap: From Noise To High-Context Intelligence | UpGuard
item.description: Bridge the context gap with high-context intelligence. Stop the manual hunt for alert context and reclaim your time with UpGuard Breach Risk.

item.title: Bad Memories Still Haunt AI Agents
item.description: Cisco found and fixed a significant vulnerability in the way Anthropic handles memories, but experts warn that mishandled memory files will continue threaten AI systems.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: Bitwarden CLI, the command-line interface for the password manager Bitwarden, has reportedly been compromised as part of a newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version appears to be @bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0, and the malicious code was published in 'bw1.js,' a file included in the package contents," the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Level Up Your Payment Security Expertise with PCI SSC Knowledge Training
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: $290M DeFi Hack, macOS LotL Abuse, ProxySmart SIM Farms +25 New Stories
item.description: You scroll past one incident and see another that feels familiar, like it should have been fixed years ago, but it still works with small changes. Same bugs. Same mistakes. The supply chain is messy. Packages you did not check are stealing data, adding backdoors, and spreading. Attacking the systems behind apps is easier than breaking the apps themselves. The exploits are simple but still work
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Fixes iOS Notification Bug Exposing Deleted Messages
item.description: Apple patches iOS flaw that retained deleted notifications, exposing message data

item.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
item.description: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated, large-scale exploits faster than ever before. The time you have to fix a vulnerability before it gets attacked is shrinking to zero. We call this the Collapsing Exploit Window, and it means your
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Introduces Unique AI Agent Identities in New Gemini Enterprise Platform
item.description: Google Cloud will attribute a unique cryptographic ID every AI agent that will be tied to “traceable and auditable” authorization policies

item.title: Project Glasswing Proved AI Can Find the Bugs. Who's Going to Fix Them?
item.description: Last week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, an AI model so effective at discovering software vulnerabilities that they took the extraordinary step of postponing its public release. Instead, the company has given access to Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and a coalition of others to find and patch bugs before adversaries can. Mythos Preview, the model that led to Project Glasswing, found
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI Extracts Deleted Signal Messages from iPhone Notification Database
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cyber-Attacks Surge 63% Annually in Education Sector
item.description: Quorum Cyber report finds higher and further education institutions experienced 63% increase in attacks over a year

item.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
item.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Researchers Uncover 10 In-the-Wild Prompt Injection Payloads Targeting AI Agents
item.description: Forcepoint has found 10 new indirect prompt injection attacks targeting AI agents

item.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
item.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide array of tools mostly written in Go, using injectors and loaders to deploy and execute various backdoors in its arsenal," Slovakian cybersecurity company ESET said in a report shared with The Hacker
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GopherWhisper: A burrow full of malware
item.description: ESET Research has discovered a new China-aligned APT group that we’ve named GopherWhisper, which targets Mongolian governmental institutions

item.title: NCSC Backs Passkeys, Hailing a New Era of Sign-in
item.description: The UK’s NCSC has fully backed passkeys as consumers’ first choice for login, citing progress with FIDO and successful use across the NHS

item.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
item.description: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The company said it made the discovery after expanding its investigation to include an extra set of compromise indicators, alongside a review of requests to the Vercel network and environment
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Fixes iOS Flaw That Let FBI Recover Deleted Signal Messages
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Africa Relinquishes Cyberattack Lead to Latin America — For Now
item.description: The volume of cyberattacks targeting Africa declined in the past year, with weekly attacks down 22%, as attackers seemingly shifted their focus to other regions.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Still Leaves 1,300 SharePoint Servers Exposed
item.description: <p>More than 1,300 internet-exposed SharePoint servers remain unpatched against CVE-2026-32201, a spoofing flaw Microsoft says was exploited as a zero-day.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-1300-sharepoint-servers-unpatched-zero-day-flaw/">Microsoft Patch Still Leaves 1,300 SharePoint Servers Exposed</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
item.description: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Vonage, Girls Who Code Show What ‘Responsible AI’ Looks Like
item.description: <p>Vonage’s partnership with Girls Who Code is more than feel-good philanthropy; it’s a blueprint for building diverse AI talent pipelines.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-vonage-girls-who-code-ai-talent-pipeline/">Vonage, Girls Who Code Show What ‘Responsible AI’ Looks Like</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Zeus Kerravala

item.title: Mozilla Fixes 271 Firefox Bugs Using Anthropic’s Mythos AI
item.description: <p>Mozilla says Firefox 150 patches 271 vulnerabilities found with Anthropic’s restricted Mythos AI, highlighting how quickly AI-driven bug hunting is accelerating.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-mozilla-firefox-150-patched-271-security-flaws/">Mozilla Fixes 271 Firefox Bugs Using Anthropic’s Mythos AI</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown threat actors managed to have overwritten existing tags, including v2.1.20 and alpine, while also introducing a new v2.1.21 tag that does not correspond to an official release. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake Google Antigravity Installer Can Steal Accounts in Minutes
item.description: <p>Fake Antigravity downloads are enabling fast account takeovers using hidden malware and stolen session cookies.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-google-antigravity-downloads-steal-accounts-minutes/">Fake Google Antigravity Installer Can Steal Accounts in Minutes</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has been detected by both Socket and StepSecurity, with the companies tracking the activity under the name CanisterSprawl owing to the use of an ICP canister to exfiltrate the stolen data
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft: Most Windows 11 Users Don’t Need Third-Party Antivirus
item.description: <p>Microsoft says Windows 11’s built-in security is strong enough for most users, though power users and enterprises may still want third-party protection.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-11-no-third-party-antivirus-needed/">Microsoft: Most Windows 11 Users Don’t Need Third-Party Antivirus</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: New Apple Phishing Scam Uses Fake $899 iPhone Purchase Alert
item.description: <p>An Apple account notification has been exploited in a new email phishing attack that comes with a fake iPhone purchase claim.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-phishing-scam-fake-899-iphone-purchase-alert/">New Apple Phishing Scam Uses Fake $899 iPhone Purchase Alert</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Esther Shein

item.title: MacOS Native Tools Enable Stealthy Enterprise Attacks
item.description: macOS LOTL techniques bypass detection using native tools and metadata abuse

item.title: Malicious TikTok Downloader Extensions Quietly Compromised 130K Users
item.description: <p>Malicious browser extensions disguised as TikTok downloaders compromised 130,000 users, exposing a growing blind spot in enterprise security.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-malicious-tiktok-downloader-extensions/">Malicious TikTok Downloader Extensions Quietly Compromised 130K Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
item.description: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control (C2) channel, allowing it to bypass traditional perimeter network defenses," the Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: NCSC Unveils SilentGlass, a Plug-In Device to Protect Monitors from Cyber-Attacks
item.description: The UK’s cybersecurity agency said the devices will be available for purchase by organizations around the world

item.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
item.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Electricity Is a Growing Area of Cyber Risk
item.description: IT has long been concerned about ensuring systems receive the right amount of electricity. Cyberattackers are realizing they can manipulate voltage fluctuations for their purposes, too.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: UK Commits £90m for Cybersecurity and Pushes for ‘Resilience Pledge’
item.description: UK unveils £90m cybersecurity funding at CYBERUK to boost SME resilience, promote Cyber Essentials and a new Cyber Resilience Pledge, sparking industry debate

item.title: Surge in Silent Subject Phishing Attacks Targets VIP Users
item.description: Null subject phishing campaigns bypass filters and target VIPs with QR code and RMM abuse

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: A Pivot To Usage-Based Discovery | UpGuard
item.description: Pivot from "purchase-based" to usage-based discovery. See how browser telemetry and identity logs work together to secure your true vendor footprint.

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: The SSO & AI Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Is your SSO enough? 31.4% of vendor interactions bypass identity logs. Discover how Shadow AI and direct logins redefine vendor visibility.

item.title: ICE Uses Graphite Spyware
item.description: <p>ICE has <a href="https://www.npr.org/2026/04/07/nx-s1-5776799/ice-spyware-privacy">admitted</a> that it uses spyware from the Israeli company Graphite.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Former Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Working For BlackCat Cyber Gang
item.description: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to abusing his position by working with noted cybercrime group BlackCat

item.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper has been used in a destructive campaign targeting the energy and utilities sector in Venezuela, per findings from Kaspersky. "Two batch scripts are responsible for initiating the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
item.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents. The more worrying part sat inside the private messages. Some of those conversations held plaintext third-party credentials, including OpenAI API keys shared between agents,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researchers Uncover ProxySmart Software Powering 90+ SIM Farms
item.description: Infrawatch says ProxySmart platform enables SIM farm activity at “industrial scale”

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0. It's rated Important in severity. An anonymous researcher has been credited with discovering and reporting the flaw. "Improper verification of cryptographic
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Faces a Cyber ‘Perfect Storm’ Driven by Tech Advances and Nation State Threats, NCSC Warns
item.description: The convergence of global tensions and rapid technological change is driving a new era of cyber risk, the NCSC warns

item.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command-and-control server over HTTPS and supports remote shell access, file operations, and session management, indicating a continued espionage-focused capability set rather than
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scoring system. "Sandbox escape vulnerability in Terrarium allows arbitrary code execution with root privileges on a host process via JavaScript prototype chain traversal," according to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
item.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
item.description: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
item.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research published by Check Point, the command-and-control (C2 or C&amp;C) server linked to SystemBC has led to the discovery of a botnet of more than 1,570 victims. "SystemBC establishes SOCKS5 network tunnels within
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Trojanized Android App Fuels New Wave of NFC Fraud
item.description: NGate malware abuses HandyPay app to steal NFC card data and PINs in Brazil

item.title: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK Flaws Expose Thousands of Lantronix and Silex Serial-to-IP Converters
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper with data exchanged by them. The vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed BRIDGE:BREAK by Forescout Research Vedere Labs, which identified nearly 20,000 Serial-to-Ethernet converters exposed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
item.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: PCI SSC Launches Enhanced Language Microsites for Global Audience
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based 'Antigravity' Tool
item.description: The prompt-injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
item.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
item.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed up with the operators of the BlackCat ransomware starting in April 2023 to assist the e-crime gang in extracting higher amounts as ransoms. "Working as a negotiator on behalf of five different
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Expands With Rapid Affiliate Growth
item.description: Gentlemen RaaS expands quickly with multi-platform attacks and SystemBC-linked infections

item.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
item.description: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. Leadership sees it differently: every hour a threat dwells inside the environment is an hour of potential data exfiltration, service disruption, regulatory exposure, and brand damage.&nbsp; The root cause of slow MTTR is almost never "not enough analysts." It is almost always the same structural problem: threat intelligence that exists
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Unchecked AI Agents Cause Cybersecurity Incidents at Two Thirds of Firms
item.description: Data exposure, operational disruption and financial losses among issues faced by businesses struggling with the rapid rise of AI agents, warns Cloud Security Alliance report

item.title: NGate Campaign Targets Brazil, Trojanizes HandyPay to Steal NFC Data and PINs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called&nbsp;NGate&nbsp;that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called&nbsp;HandyPay instead of NFCGate. "The threat actors took the app, which is used to relay NFC data, and patched it with malicious code that appears to have been AI-generated," ESET security researcher Lukáš Štefanko said in a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
item.description: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
item.description: The cybersecurity industry has spent the last several years chasing sophisticated threats like zero-days, supply chain compromises, and AI-generated exploits. However, the most reliable entry point for attackers still hasn't changed: stolen credentials. Identity-based attacks remain a dominant initial access vector in breaches today. Attackers obtain valid credentials through credential stuffing
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mexican Surveillance Company
item.description: <p><a href="https://restofworld.org/2026/mexico-seguritech-government-surveillance-profile/">Grupo Seguritech</a> is a Mexican surveillance company that is expanding into the US.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Google Patches Antigravity IDE Flaw Enabling Prompt Injection Code Execution
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a vulnerability in Google's agentic integrated development environment (IDE), Antigravity, that could be exploited to achieve code execution. The flaw, since patched, combines Antigravity's permitted file-creation capabilities with an insufficient input sanitization in Antigravity's native file-searching tool, find_by_name, to bypass the program's Strict
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Vercel Confirms Cyber Incident After Sophisticated Attacker Exploits Third‑Party Tool
item.description: Cloud app developer Vercel appears to have suffered a security breach

item.title: New NGate variant hides in a trojanized NFC payment app
item.description: ESET researchers discover another iteration of NGate malware, this time possibly developed with the assistance of AI

item.title: North Korea Blamed for $290m KelpDAO Crypto Heist
item.description: North Korea’s Lazarus Group is pegged for a $290m crypto theft at KelpDAO

item.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added eight new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, including three flaws impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -
CVE-2023-27351 (CVSS score: 8.2) - An improper authentication vulnerability in PaperCut
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
item.description: Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, &quot;are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement,&quot; a researcher notes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Serial-to-IP Devices Hide Thousands of Old &amp; New Bugs
item.description: The OT devices that translate machine talk into Internet-speak are riddled with vulnerabilities and more frequently targeted for attacks, researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Amtrak Data Breach Exposes 2.1M Records, Reports Suggest Larger Leak
item.description: <p>Amtrak data breach exposes over 2.1 million customer records after CRM access. Learn what was leaked, risks, and steps users and IT teams should take now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-amtrak-data-breach-2-1m-records/">Amtrak Data Breach Exposes 2.1M Records, Reports Suggest Larger Leak</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco
